L1.area: Data type conversion and raw memory manipulation routines

L2.description: A buffer overflow vulnerability exists due to incorrect ordering of size parameters when constructing a container from raw memory during type conversion operations. This can lead to heap memory corruption or data leakage.

L3.area: Data type conversion and raw memory manipulation routines
L3.description: A buffer overflow vulnerability exists due to incorrect ordering of size parameters when constructing a container from raw memory during type conversion operations. This can lead to heap memory corruption or data leakage.

ground_truth.reason: The length and capacity arguments are swapped when constructing a vector from raw parts during type transmutation, leading to heap memory corruption or out-of-bounds access.
